# Baskets

> Single-tenant project management app (workspaces → projects → tasks → sub-tasks).
> This file documents the REST API so agents can drive Baskets programmatically.
> Base URL = this deployment's origin (e.g. http://localhost:5173). All API paths below are relative to it.

## Authentication

Every `/api/*` endpoint requires auth. Use a per-user API key as a bearer token:

```
Authorization: Bearer bsk_<token>
```

- The header must start literally with `Bearer bsk_`; anything else is treated as unauthenticated → `401`.
- Create keys in the web UI at `/settings` (API keys section). The plaintext key is shown exactly ONCE at creation; only its SHA-256 hash is stored. Format: `bsk_` + random token.
- A key inherits its owner's access. Banned users are rejected.
- The browser session cookie is also accepted (used by the web UI); agents should use a bearer key.

## Conventions

- Request bodies are JSON objects (`Content-Type: application/json`). A non-object body → `400 {"error":"Invalid JSON body"}`.
- All errors: `{ "error": "<message>" }` with the documented status code.
- `401 {"error":"Unauthorized"}` when auth is missing/invalid.
- Visibility: a project (or its tasks) the caller cannot access returns `404` — identical to a truly missing resource (never `403` on read), so inaccessible and nonexistent are indistinguishable.
- Timestamps in responses are ISO-8601 strings (e.g. `"2026-06-21T00:00:00.000Z"`); a nullable date is `null` when unset. Booleans are JSON `true`/`false`.
- Priority enum: `none | low | medium | high | urgent` (default `none`).
- Status category (drives behavior, not the name): `backlog | planned | in-progress | completed | canceled` (`completed` = the "done" bucket that completes sub-tasks; `backlog` = new-task default).
- Icon values (status/label/option/project `icon` fields): an emoji/glyph string, OR `iconoir:<name>` where `<name>` is a **Heroicon** name (24px outline; the `iconoir:` prefix is a historical namespace kept for back-compat — ADR-052). Unknown/invalid names → `400 "Unknown icon"`; `null`/`""` clears.

## Session

### GET /api/me
Identity of the current session/key holder.
- 200: `{ "user": { "id": string, "name": string } }`
- 401 if unauthenticated.

## Realtime

The web UI receives live updates and presence over a WebSocket at `/ws` (same origin), authenticated by the **browser session cookie** only — it is not part of the bearer-key REST surface. After any mutation through the API or UI, connected viewers of the affected project auto-refresh. Agents using a bearer key don't need to connect; just poll the REST endpoints. Messages are JSON: client→server `{type:'subscribe',projectId}` / `{type:'unsubscribe'}` / `{type:'ping'}`; server→client `{type:'changed',projectId}` (refetch) and `{type:'presence',projectId,users:[{id,name}]}`.

## Workspaces

Every project belongs to one workspace. A workspace owns its custom statuses and ALL labels/groups, and (per ADR-019) defines visibility. Workspace meta + structure edits need admin, the workspace owner, or a workspace `permission` grant (`canEditWorkspace`); reads need access (`canAccessWorkspace`). Inaccessible workspaces 404, not 403.

### GET /api/workspaces
List accessible workspaces (admins see all; others see ones they own / hold a grant on, plus any workspace holding a project they're directly granted).
- 200: `{ "workspaces": [ <workspace>, ... ] }`

### POST /api/workspaces
Create a workspace (any signed-in user; they become its owner).
- Body: `name` string, required (trimmed, non-empty, max 120). `400 "A workspace with that name already exists"` on a case-insensitive name collision.
- The caller's FIRST workspace is auto-seeded with sample projects/milestones/tasks.
- 201: `{ "workspace": <workspace> }`. Errors: 400, 401.

### GET /api/workspaces/{id}
Fetch one workspace.
- 200: `{ "workspace": <workspace> }`. `404` if missing or inaccessible.

### PATCH /api/workspaces/{id}
Rename a workspace.
- Body: `name` string, required (else `400 "No fields to update"`; trimmed non-empty, max 120; `400` on name collision).
- 200: `{ "workspace": <workspace> }`. `404` if missing/inaccessible; `403 "No edit permission on this workspace"` if accessible but not editable.

### DELETE /api/workspaces/{id}
Delete an EMPTY workspace.
- `400 "Move or delete its projects first"` if it still has projects; `400 "At least one workspace must exist"` if it's the only one.
- `404` if missing/inaccessible; `403` if accessible but not editable. 204: empty body.

### GET /api/workspaces/{id}/statuses
List the workspace's own custom statuses (access only).
- 200: `{ "statuses": [<status>...] }`. `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/workspaces/{id}/statuses
Create a workspace-scoped status (editable).
- Body: `name` (required, trimmed, max 40), `description` (string|null, max 200), `category` (one of the status categories, default `backlog`; `400` otherwise), `color` (`#rrggbb` hex or null), `icon` (emoji or `iconoir:<name>`, else null). `400 "A status with that name already exists here"` if the name collides with defaults, workspace siblings, or any of its projects' statuses.
- 201: `{ "status": <status> }`. `404`/`403` as for workspace edits.

### PATCH /api/workspaces/{id}/statuses
Reorder the workspace's custom statuses.
- Body: `order` — array listing EXACTLY this workspace's status ids (`400` otherwise). Customs are kept positioned after the built-in defaults.
- 200: `{ "statuses": [<status>...] }`.

### GET /api/workspaces/{id}/labels
List the workspace's labels (access only).
- 200: `{ "labels": [<label>...] }`. `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/workspaces/{id}/labels
Create a workspace label (editable).
- Body: `name` (required, trimmed, max 40; `400 "A label with that name exists"` on collision), `color` (`#rrggbb` or null), `icon` (emoji/`iconoir:<name>` or null), `groupId` (string|null — must be a group of this workspace, else `400 "Unknown group"`).
- 201: `{ "label": <label> }`. `404`/`403` as for workspace edits.

### GET /api/workspaces/{id}/label-groups
List the workspace's label groups (access only).
- 200: `{ "groups": [<labelGroup>...] }`. `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/workspaces/{id}/label-groups
Create a label group (editable).
- Body: `name` (required, trimmed, max 40; `400 "A group with that name exists"` on collision).
- 201: `{ "group": <labelGroup> }`. `404`/`403` as for workspace edits.

### GET /api/workspaces/{id}/grants
List the workspace's permission grants. Managed by ADMINS or the workspace OWNER only.
- 200: `{ "grants": [{ "id", "userId", "userName", "resourceType" ("workspace"), "resourceId" }...] }`. `404` if inaccessible; `403 "Only admins or the owner can view workspace grants"` for accessible non-managers.

### POST /api/workspaces/{id}/grants
Grant a user access to the workspace (admin or owner).
- Body: `userId` string, required (`400 "Invalid grant"` if missing, `400 "Unknown user"` if no such user). Idempotent (conflicts are ignored).
- 201: `{ "grants": [...] }` (the updated list). `404`/`403` as above.

### DELETE /api/workspaces/{id}/grants
Revoke a grant (admin or owner). Identify it by `grantId` OR `userId`, via JSON body or query string (`?grantId=` / `?userId=`); `400 "Provide grantId or userId"` if neither.
- 204: empty body. `404`/`403` as above.

## Statuses

Status definitions are table-driven and scoped (built-in app-wide / workspace / project). Create workspace statuses via `POST /api/workspaces/{id}/statuses`, project statuses via `POST /api/projects/{id}/statuses`. The endpoints below edit/delete an existing status by id.

### PATCH /api/statuses/{id}
Update a status. Gating depends on the status's scope:
- **Built-in app-wide default** (no project/workspace): ADMIN only (`403 "Admins only"`); ONLY its `icon` is settable (`400 "Pick an icon"` if absent).
- **Workspace-scoped**: needs workspace edit (`404` if inaccessible, `403` otherwise).
- **Project-scoped**: needs project edit (`404` if inaccessible, `403` otherwise).
- Editable fields (custom statuses): `name` (trimmed, max 40; `400` on collision), `description` (string|null, max 200), `category`, `color` (`#rrggbb`|null), `icon`. At least one (else `400 "No fields to update"`).
- 200: `{ "status": <status> }`. `404 "Status not found"` if missing.

### DELETE /api/statuses/{id}
Delete a custom status. `400 "Built-in statuses cannot be deleted"` for defaults; `400 "Status is used by N task(s)"` if any task uses it. Gating by scope (as PATCH). `404` if missing/inaccessible; `403` if not editable. 204: empty body.

## Labels

Workspace labels are created via `POST /api/workspaces/{id}/labels`; project-scoped labels via `POST /api/projects/{id}/labels`. The endpoints here edit/delete an existing label (workspace OR project scoped) by id; gating follows the label's owner (`404` if inaccessible, `403` if not editable).

### PATCH /api/labels/{id}
Update a label. Body: `name` (trimmed, max 40; uniqueness scoped to its owner; `400` on collision), `color` (`#rrggbb`|null), `icon` (emoji/`iconoir:<name>`|null). At least one (else `400 "No fields to update"`).
- 200: `{ "label": <label> }`.

### DELETE /api/labels/{id}
Delete a label (also drops it from its tasks/projects). 204: empty body.

### DELETE /api/label-groups/{id}
Delete a workspace label group (workspace edit). Its labels survive (their `groupId` is cleared). `404` if missing/inaccessible; `403` if not editable. 204: empty body.

## Project labels

The project-facing label pool (workspace pool ∪ this project's own labels) and attachment of workspace labels onto a project.

### GET /api/projects/{id}/labels
List the labels available to a project (access only).
- 200: `{ "labels": [<label>...], "workspaceLabels": [...], "projectLabels": [...], "attachedLabelIds": [string...] }`. `available` = project-scoped labels (always available) + the workspace pool. `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/projects/{id}/labels
Create a PROJECT-scoped label (project edit).
- Body: `name` (required, trimmed, max 40; `400 "A label with that name exists"` on collision among this project's labels), `color` (`#rrggbb`|null), `icon`.
- 201: `{ "label": <label> }`. `404`/`403` as for project edits.

### PATCH (or PUT) /api/projects/{id}/labels
Toggle a WORKSPACE label onto/off this project (via `project_label`). Project edit.
- Body: `labelId` string, required (`400 "Unknown label"` if it isn't a label of this project's workspace).
- 200: `{ "labelId": string, "attached": boolean }` (the new membership state).

## Milestones

Milestones are per-project; create via `POST /api/projects/{id}/milestones`. CRUD + dependencies need project edit; reads need access (`404` if inaccessible).

### GET /api/projects/{id}/milestones
List a project's milestones (position, then createdAt).
- 200: `{ "milestones": [<milestone>...] }`. `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/projects/{id}/milestones
Create a milestone (project edit).
- Body: `name` (required, trimmed, max 120), `description` (string|null), `startDate` (string|null — `YYYY-MM-DD`/ISO), `targetDate` (string|null). Bad dates → `400`.
- 201: `{ "milestone": <milestone> }`. `404`/`403` as for project edits.

### PATCH /api/projects/{id}/milestones
Reorder this project's milestones (project edit). Body: `order` (array of milestone ids). Foreign ids are ignored; any of the project's milestones omitted from the list keep their relative order after the listed ones. Rewrites `milestone.position`.
- 200: `{ "success": true }`. `404`/`403` as for project edits.

### PATCH /api/milestones/{id}
Update a milestone (project edit). Body: `name` (trimmed non-empty, max 120), `description` (string|null), `startDate` (string|null), `targetDate` (string|null). At least one (else `400 "No fields to update"`).
- 200: `{ "milestone": <milestone> }`. `404` if missing/inaccessible; `403` if not editable.

### DELETE /api/milestones/{id}
Delete a milestone (project edit). Tasks keep their row but lose the ref. 204: empty body.

### GET /api/milestones/{id}/dependencies
List the milestone ids this milestone depends on (access only).
- 200: `{ "dependsOnIds": [string...] }`. `404` if missing/inaccessible.

### POST /api/milestones/{id}/dependencies
Add ONE dependency (project edit). Body: `dependsOnId` string, required. `400` if it's the milestone itself, not a milestone of the same project, or would create a cycle (DFS-checked).
- 201: `{ "success": true }`.

### PUT /api/milestones/{id}/dependencies
Replace the FULL dependency set (project edit). Body: `dependsOnIds` array (`400` if not an array). Invalid ids, self-refs, and cycle-forming edges are silently dropped.
- 200: `{ "dependsOnIds": [string...] }` (the accepted set).

### DELETE /api/milestones/{id}/dependencies?dependsOnId={id}
Remove one dependency edge (project edit; `dependsOnId` query param required). 204: empty body.

## Locations

Project-scoped saved places; create via `POST /api/projects/{id}/locations`. CRUD needs project edit (picking one onto a task only needs task edit, via PATCH `/api/tasks`).

### GET /api/projects/{id}/locations
List a project's locations (position, then title). 200: `{ "locations": [<location>...] }`. `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/projects/{id}/locations
Create a location (project edit).
- Body: `title` (required, trimmed, max 120), `address` (string|null, max 240), `latitude` (number|string|null, -90…90), `longitude` (number|string|null, -180…180). Out-of-range coords → `400 "<field> must be between …"`.
- 201: `{ "location": <location> }`. `404`/`403` as for project edits.

### PATCH /api/locations/{id}
Update a location (project edit). Body: `title` (trimmed non-empty, max 120), `address` (string|null, max 240), `latitude`, `longitude` (ranges as above). At least one (else `400 "No fields to update"`).
- 200: `{ "location": <location> }`.

### DELETE /api/locations/{id}
Delete a location (project edit). Tasks referencing it keep their row (FK set null). 204: empty body.

## Views

One project view per row (table/board/list/timeline/calendar/dashboard/map/flow — flow is a Svelte Flow dependency graph of tasks + milestones, display-only). Create/reorder need project edit; per-view PATCH/DELETE need `canEditView` (cascades from project edit). Reads need project access.

### GET /api/projects/{id}/views
List a project's views (position, then createdAt). 200: `{ "views": [<view>...] }`. `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/projects/{id}/views
Create a view, OR duplicate one (project edit).
- **Create** body: `type` (one of the view types, required; `400` otherwise), `name` (optional — trimmed non-empty, max 120, unique-per-project; auto-derived from the type if omitted). New views start with `config = "{}"`.
- **Duplicate** body: `duplicateViewId` (a view id of this project; copies its type + config; the name gets a " copy"/" copy N" suffix).
- 201: `{ "view": <view> }`. `404`/`403` as for project edits.

### PATCH /api/projects/{id}/views
Reorder views (project edit). Body: `order` — a non-empty array of this project's view ids (`400` otherwise).
- 200: `{ "views": [<view>...] }`.

### PATCH /api/views/{id}
Update one view (view edit). Body (at least one, else `400 "No fields to update"`): `name` (trimmed non-empty, max 120), `config` (a JSON object OR a JSON-object string — `400` if it isn't valid JSON / isn't an object; stored stringified), `hidden` (boolean — hiding the last visible view is rejected with `400 "A project must keep at least one visible view"`).
- 200: `{ "view": <view> }`. `404` if missing/inaccessible; `403 "No edit permission on this view"` otherwise.

### DELETE /api/views/{id}
Delete a view (view edit). `400 "A project must keep at least one view"` if it's the last visible one (a hidden view may always be removed). `404`/`403` as PATCH. 204: empty body.

## Custom field definitions

CRUD for a project's custom-field DEFINITIONS + their select options. Field/option create + reorder + edit + delete need project edit; reads need project access (`404` if inaccessible). Field `type` is IMMUTABLE after creation. (Task VALUES are still written via the `customFields` map on `/api/tasks`; project-entity VALUES via `/api/projects/{id}/custom-values`.)

### GET /api/projects/{id}/custom-fields
List a project's field definitions (config parsed, display order).
- 200: `{ "customFields": [<customField>...] }`. `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/projects/{id}/custom-fields
Create a field definition (project edit).
- Body: `name` (required, trimmed, max 60; `400 "A field with that name already exists"` on collision), `type` (a valid custom-field type, else `400 "Invalid field type"`), `appliesTo` (`all|tasks|subtasks`, default `all`; `400 "Invalid appliesTo"`), `entity` (`task` default | `project`), `config` (a JSON object or JSON string; canonicalized for the type).
- 201: `{ "customField": <customField> }` (with `config` as a parsed object). `404`/`403` as for project edits.

### PATCH /api/projects/{id}/custom-fields
Reorder field definitions (project edit). Body: `order` — array listing EVERY field of this project exactly once (`400` otherwise).
- 200: `{ "customFields": [<customField>...] }`.

### PATCH /api/custom-fields/{id}
Update one field definition (project edit). Body: `name` (trimmed, max 60, unique), `appliesTo` (`all|tasks|subtasks`), `config` (JSON object/string, re-validated for the field's type). `type` is rejected if it differs (`400 "type is immutable after creation"`). At least one (else `400 "No fields to update"`).
- 200: `{ "customField": <customField> }`. `404` if missing/inaccessible; `403` otherwise.

### DELETE /api/custom-fields/{id}
Delete a field definition (project edit). For a `files` field its uploaded bytes are unlinked first. `404`/`403` as PATCH. 204: empty body.

### POST /api/custom-fields/{id}/options
Add a `select` option (project edit; `400 "Not a select field"` for other types).
- Body: `title` (required, trimmed, max 60), `color` (`#rrggbb`|null), `icon`.
- 201: `{ "option": <customFieldOption> }`. `404` if the field is missing/inaccessible; `403` otherwise.

### PATCH /api/custom-fields/{id}/options
Reorder a field's options (project edit). Body: `order` — array listing EVERY option of this field exactly once (`400` otherwise).
- 200: `{ "options": [<customFieldOption>...] }`.

### PATCH /api/custom-field-options/{id}
Update one option (project edit). Body: `title` (trimmed non-empty, max 60), `color` (`#rrggbb`|null), `icon`. At least one (else `400 "No fields to update"`).
- 200: `{ "option": <customFieldOption> }`. `404`/`403` by the option's project.

### DELETE /api/custom-field-options/{id}
Delete an option (project edit). Its id is stripped from every task value of the field first (rows that become empty are removed). `404`/`403` by the option's project. 204: empty body.

## Project custom values

Read/write the project's OWN (entity=`project`) custom-field values. Reads need project access; writes need project edit.

### GET /api/projects/{id}/custom-values
- 200: `{ "values": { "<fieldId>": decodedValue, ... } }` (only `entity:'project'` fields). `404` if inaccessible.

### PATCH /api/projects/{id}/custom-values
Set project-entity field values (project edit).
- Body: `customFields` — a `{ "<fieldId>": value }` map (required, else `400 "customFields object is required"`); each value validated against its field; a validation failure → `400` and nothing is written.
- 200: `{ "values": { ... } }` (the refreshed map).

## Dependencies

Informational, DFS cycle-checked dependency edges between projects and between tasks. Adds/removes need edit; reads need access. (Milestone dependencies are under **Milestones**.)

### GET /api/projects/{id}/dependencies
List the project ids this project depends on (access only). 200: `{ "dependsOn": [string...] }`. `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/projects/{id}/dependencies
Add a project dependency (project edit). Body: `dependsOnId` string. `400 "Invalid dependency"` if empty/self; `400 "Unknown project"` if the target is missing or inaccessible (no cross-project oracle); `400` if it would create a cycle.
- 201: `{ "dependsOn": [string...] }`.

### DELETE /api/projects/{id}/dependencies?dependsOnId={id}
Remove a project dependency (project edit; `dependsOnId` query param required). 204: empty body.

### GET /api/tasks/{id}/dependencies
List the task ids this task depends on (project access only). 200: `{ "dependsOnIds": [string...] }`. `404` if missing/inaccessible.

### POST /api/tasks/{id}/dependencies
Add a task dependency (task edit). Body: `dependsOnId` string, required. `400` if it's the task itself, not a task of the same project, a cross-level link (sub-tasks may only depend on sibling sub-tasks of the same parent), or would create a cycle.
- 201: `{ "dependsOnIds": [string...] }`. `404` if missing/inaccessible; `403` if not editable.

### DELETE /api/tasks/{id}/dependencies?dependsOnId={id}
Remove a task dependency (task edit; `dependsOnId` query param required). 204: empty body.

## Task labels

A task's attached labels. Read needs project access; writes need task edit (`404` if missing/inaccessible, `403` if not editable). A label is valid only if it's a workspace label of the task's project's workspace OR scoped to the task's project.

### GET /api/tasks/{id}/labels
- 200: `{ "labelIds": [string...] }`. `404` if missing/inaccessible.

### POST /api/tasks/{id}/labels
Attach one label (task edit). Body: `labelId` string, required (`400 "Unknown label"` if invalid). Idempotent.
- 200: `{ "labelIds": [string...] }`.

### PUT /api/tasks/{id}/labels
Replace the FULL label set (task edit). Body: `labelIds` array of strings (deduped; `400` if not an array of strings, `400 "Too many labels (max 100)"`, `400 "Unknown label: <id>"` for any invalid id).
- 200: `{ "labelIds": [string...] }`.

### DELETE /api/tasks/{id}/labels?labelId={id}
Detach one label (task edit; `labelId` query param required). 204: empty body.

## Bulk task actions

Operate on a SELECTION of tasks (`ids`), which must all belong to ONE project (`400 "All ids must belong to the same project"`). Inaccessible ids are silently dropped (ADR-019); if none remain → `404 "No tasks found"`. Only tasks the caller can edit are mutated.

### PATCH /api/tasks/bulk
Set fields on every editable selected task.
- Body: `ids` (non-empty string array) + `set` (object). `set` may include: `statusId` or `status` (name; must be eligible in the project), `assigneeId` (string|null; validated user), `milestoneId` (string|null; same project), `priority` (priority enum), `parentId` (string|null — re-parent under a top-level task; `""`/null promotes to top-level; `400` if it targets a selected id, a non-top-level task, or a task with sub-tasks), `addLabelIds` / `removeLabelIds` (string arrays — labels are multi-value, so these ADD / REMOVE the given labels across the whole selection; every id must be a label available to the project [its workspace's labels OR a project-scoped label], else `400`). At least one effective field (else `400 "No fields to update"`).
- Setting a `completed`-category status cascades to the selected tasks' sub-tasks.
- 200: `{ "success": true, "updated": number }`.

### POST /api/tasks/bulk
Create a new top-level task and re-parent the selection under it (Move → create).
- Body: `ids` (non-empty array) + `title` (required, trimmed, max 240). Selected tasks must be childless (`400 "cannot move a task that has sub-tasks"`).
- 201: `{ "success": true, "parentId": string, "moved": number }`.

### DELETE /api/tasks/bulk
Delete every deletable selected task (and their sub-tasks).
- Body: `ids` (non-empty array). 204: empty body.

## API keys

The caller's own API keys. Owner-scoped; the token/hash are NEVER returned (the plaintext key is shown exactly ONCE, at creation).

### GET /api/keys
List the caller's keys (no token/hash).
- 200: `{ "keys": [{ "id", "name", "prefix", "lastUsedAt", "createdAt" }...] }` (newest first).

### POST /api/keys
Create a key. Body: `name` string, required (trimmed, max 60).
- 201: `{ "key": { "id", "name", "prefix", "lastUsedAt" (null), "createdAt" }, "token": "bsk_…" }`. The `token` is the only time the plaintext is returned.

### DELETE /api/keys
Delete one of the caller's keys. Body: `id` string, required (`400 "Missing key id"`). A key the caller doesn't own is indistinguishable from missing → `404 "Key not found"`. 204: empty body.

## Projects

### GET /api/projects
List accessible projects, newest first.
- 200: `{ "projects": [ <project>, ... ] }`

### POST /api/projects
Create a project.
- Body:
  - `name` string, required. Trimmed, non-empty, max 120.
  - `description` string|null, optional.
  - `workspaceId` string|null, optional. Must exist if given (`400 "unknown workspaceId"`); if omitted, defaults to the OLDEST workspace (or `400 "no workspace exists yet"` if none exist at all). Requires edit rights on the chosen workspace (`403 "No permission to create projects in this workspace"`).
- 201: `{ "project": <project> }`
- Errors: 400 (name/description/workspaceId), 401, 403.

### GET /api/projects/{id}
Fetch one project with nested resources.
- 200: `{ "project": <project>, "tasks": [<task>...], "views": [<view>...], "milestones": [<milestone>...], "locations": [<location>...], "statuses": [<status>...], "customFields": [<customField>...], "customFieldOptions": [<customFieldOption>...] }` (`statuses` = the project's eligible statuses; each task carries its decoded `customFields` map — see Object shapes).
- 404 if missing or inaccessible.

### PATCH /api/projects/{id}
Update project metadata. At least one field required (else `400 "No fields to update"`).
- Body (any of):
  - `name` string, trimmed non-empty, max 120.
  - `description` string|null.
  - `pinned` boolean (`400` if not a boolean) — sidebar pin / sort-first.
  - `icon` string|null — an emoji / glyph (≤8 chars; longer is truncated) or `iconoir:<name>` (a Heroicon name; see Conventions → Icon values; `400 "Unknown icon"` if unknown). `null`/`""` clears it.
  - `statusId` string|null — the project's own status (validated against the built-in defaults + this project's workspace statuses; `400 "Status not available to this project"` otherwise; no FK). `null`/`""` clears.
  - `statusDisplay` `text|icon|text-icon` (`400 "Invalid status display"` otherwise) — the project-wide FALLBACK default for how status pills render; per-view overrides live in `view.config.statusDisplay`.
  - `chipFields` string[]|null — ordered list of project-entity (`entity='project'`) custom-field ids shown as chips on the project page header. Unknown/foreign ids are dropped; `null` clears it (→ show all project fields that have a value). Stored as a JSON-array string and returned that way on the project object.
  - `startDate` / `dueDate` string|null — `YYYY-MM-DD` (parsed at midnight; `400 "Invalid startDate"` / `"Invalid dueDate"` on a bad date). `null`/`""` clears.
- `404` if missing OR inaccessible; `403 "No edit permission on this project"` only if accessible but the caller can't edit it.
- 200: `{ "project": <project> }`

### DELETE /api/projects/{id}
Delete a project (cascades to its tasks, views, milestones).
- `404` if missing or inaccessible; `403` only if accessible but not editable.
- 204: empty body.

## Tasks

### GET /api/tasks?projectId={id}
List a project's tasks (top-level + sub-tasks), ordered by position.
- `projectId` query param required (`400` if absent). `404` if the project is inaccessible/missing.
- 200: `{ "tasks": [<task>...] }`

### POST /api/tasks
Create a task or sub-task.
- Body:
  - `projectId` string, required.
  - `title` string, required. Trimmed, non-empty, max 240.
  - `priority` string, optional, default `none`. Must be in the priority enum.
  - `parentId` string|null, optional. If set, the parent must be a top-level task in the same project. Only one nesting level — sub-tasks cannot have children.
  - `statusId` string, optional — an eligible status id. Takes precedence over `status`.
  - `status` string, optional — an eligible status NAME (case-insensitive). Use this OR `statusId`.
    If neither is given, defaults to the first `backlog`-category status.
  - `description` string|null, optional.
  - `assigneeId` string|null, optional — must reference a valid user.
  - `milestoneId` string|null, optional — must reference a milestone of the same project.
  - `locationId` string|null, optional — must reference a saved location of the same project.
  - `dueDate` string|null, optional — `YYYY-MM-DD` (treated as midnight) or full ISO datetime.
  - `location` string|null, optional — free text `"lat, lng"` (plotted by map views).
  - `order` integer|null, optional — user rank for list views; null = unranked.
  - `customFields` object, optional — a `{ "<fieldId>": value }` map (see Custom fields). Each value is validated against its field's type; invalid → `400` (task not created).
- 201: `{ "task": <task> }` (the task includes its decoded `customFields` map).
- Any signed-in user with project access can create tasks. `404` if `projectId` is missing or inaccessible (indistinguishable, per ADR-019); `400` on validation errors.

### GET /api/tasks/{id}
Fetch one task and its sub-tasks.
- 200: `{ "task": <task>, "subTasks": [<task>...] }` (the task includes its decoded `customFields` map).
- `404` if missing or its project is inaccessible.

### PATCH /api/tasks/{id}
Update a task. At least one field (else `400 "No fields to update"`).
- Body: any of `title` (non-empty, max 240), `statusId` or `status` (name), `priority`, `description`, `assigneeId` (string|null), `milestoneId` (string|null, same project), `locationId` (string|null, same project), `location` (string|null, legacy text), `order` (integer|null), `dueDate` (string|null), `startDate` (string|null — `YYYY-MM-DD` or full ISO), `recurrence` (string|null — a repeat-rule string, validated; `400 "Invalid recurrence rule"` if malformed; `null`/`""` clears), `coverFileId` (string|null — must reference an IMAGE file attached to THIS task, else `400`; `null`/`""` clears), `parentId` (string|null — re-parent this task), `customFields` (`{ "<fieldId>": value }` map; see Custom fields — an empty/null value clears the field).
- `parentId`: move the task under another top-level task of the SAME project (makes it a sub-task), or `null`/`""` to detach it back to top-level. Rejected (`400`) if the target is itself a sub-task (only one nesting level), if it's the task itself, or if this task already has sub-tasks of its own.
- Done-cascade: setting a parent to a `completed`-category status cascades that status to all its sub-tasks. If the task has a `recurrence` rule, completing it ALSO spawns the next occurrence (a fresh backlog-status copy with the next due date).
- 200: `{ "task": <task> }`
- `404` if missing or its project is inaccessible; `403 "No edit permission on this task"` if accessible but the caller can't edit it.

### DELETE /api/tasks/{id}
Delete a task and its sub-tasks.
- `404` if missing or inaccessible; `403` only if accessible but not editable.
- 204: empty body.

## Custom fields

Projects define custom fields. Each task stores a value per field. Field definitions + their select options are returned by `GET /api/projects/{id}` and managed via the REST endpoints under **Custom field definitions** (below). Task values appear as the per-task `customFields` map on task responses and are written via the `customFields` map on POST/PATCH `/api/tasks`; PROJECT-entity field values are read/written via `GET`/`PATCH /api/projects/{id}/custom-values`.

Value encoding in the `customFields` map (key = field id):
- `text` / `email` / `phone` / `url`: string. (email/phone/url are format-validated.)
- `number`: number (or numeric string). Stored canonically; rendered per the field's number format.
- `checkbox`: boolean.
- `date`: ISO string (`YYYY-MM-DD`, or full ISO datetime when the field shows time).
- `select`: array of option ids (single-select = 1-element array). Option ids must belong to the field.
- `person`: array of user ids.
- `place`: array of `location` ids (same project).
- `task`: array of task ids (same project). A single-link field (no `config.multi`) keeps just the first id; with `config.multi` it links several. A legacy bare-id string is still accepted and read back as a 1-element array.
- `files`: array of `file` ids (upload first via `/api/files`).
- An empty array / null / "" clears the value.

Type is immutable after a field is created. There is no sort/group/filter by custom fields via the API (display only).

## Files

Files for `files`-type custom fields are stored on the server's local disk and served only through these endpoints.

### POST /api/files
Upload a file (multipart/form-data, NOT JSON).
- Form parts: `fieldId` (required, a `files`-type field), `file` (required), `taskId` (optional — must belong to the field's project, else `400`). The project is derived from the field.
- Limits: max 10 MB (`413`); executable + active-markup extensions (svg, html, xml, js, exe, …) are rejected (`415`). The stored `mimeType` is **server-derived from the extension** (the client-claimed MIME is ignored).
- `400` if no `file` part is provided, the file is empty (0 bytes), or `fieldId` is missing / does not reference a `files`-type field.
- 201: `{ "file": { "id": string, "filename": string, "mimeType": string, "size": number } }`. Add the returned `id` to the task's `files` field value (PATCH `/api/tasks/{id}` with `customFields`).
- `404` if the field's project is inaccessible.

### GET /api/files/{id}
Download the file bytes (`404` if missing, inaccessible, or the bytes are no longer on disk). Sent with `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` + `Content-Security-Policy: sandbox`; raster images stream `inline`, everything else `attachment`.

### DELETE /api/files/{id}
Delete a file (row + disk bytes) and strip its id from any task OR project custom-field value referencing it. `200: { "success": true }` on delete; `404` if missing/inaccessible.

## Task attachments

Files can also be attached DIRECTLY to a task (no custom field). These rows have `taskId` set and `fieldId` null, and back the task gallery / cover image.

### GET /api/tasks/{id}/files
List the files attached directly to a task (excludes custom-field files).
- 200: `{ "files": [{ "id", "taskId", "fieldId", "filename", "mimeType", "size" }...] }`. `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if the task or its project is inaccessible.

### POST /api/tasks/{id}/files
Upload a file straight onto a task (multipart/form-data; part `file`). The project is derived from the task; max 10 MB (`413`); blocked executable/active-markup extensions rejected (`415`); same server-derived MIME as `/api/files`.
- 201: `{ "file": { "id", "taskId", "fieldId" (null), "filename", "mimeType", "size" } }`. `400` if no `file` part or the file is empty; `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if the task is missing/inaccessible. A task attachment is task DATA, so it needs project ACCESS (not project-structure edit rights), consistent with editing a task; inaccessible ≡ missing (`404`, never `403`).
- Set a task's cover image from one of these via the `?/setTaskCover` form action (`id`, `coverFileId`; image of the same task; empty clears).

## Project files

The project Files page aggregates every file in a project — task attachments, custom-field uploads, and project-level files (rows with both `taskId` and `fieldId` null).

### GET /api/projects/{id}/files
List every file belonging to a project, newest first.
- 200: `{ "files": [{ "id", "taskId", "fieldId", "filename", "mimeType", "size", "createdAt", "createdBy" }...] }` (the on-disk path is never exposed). `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if the project is inaccessible.

### POST /api/projects/{id}/files
Upload a project-level file not tied to a task or custom field (multipart/form-data; part `file`). Max 10 MB (`413`); blocked executable/active-markup extensions rejected (`415`); same server-derived MIME as `/api/files`.
- 201: `{ "file": { "id", "filename", "mimeType", "size" } }`. `400` if no `file` part or the file is empty; `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if the project is inaccessible; `403` if accessible but the caller cannot edit the project.

## Comments

### GET /api/tasks/{id}/comments
List a task's comments plus its activity feed (newest-relevant order).
- 200: `{ "comments": [{ "id", "taskId", "authorId", "authorName", "body", "createdAt", "updatedAt", "edited" }...], "activity": [{ "id", "projectId", "taskId" (nullable), "actorId", "actorName", "type", "data", "createdAt" }...] }`. `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/tasks/{id}/comments
Add a comment.
- Body (JSON): `{ "body": string }` (non-empty, max 10000).
- 201: `{ "comment": <comment> }`. `400 "body is required"` if missing/empty, `400 "body too long (max 10000)"` if over 10000; `404` if inaccessible.

### PATCH /api/comments/{id}
Edit a comment body (author or admin only).
- Body (JSON): `{ "body": string }` (non-empty, max 10000).
- 200: `{ "comment": <comment> }`. `400` if the body is invalid JSON, empty, or over 10000 chars; `403` if not the author/admin, `404` if missing.

### DELETE /api/comments/{id}
Delete a comment (author or admin only). `403`/`404` as above. 204: empty body.

## Notifications

In-app notifications for the current user (assignment + due-date reminders). Due reminders are generated lazily on GET.

### GET /api/notifications
List the current user's notifications + unread count.
- 200: `{ "notifications": [{ "id", "userId", "type", "body", "projectId", "taskId", "read" (boolean), "createdAt" }...], "unreadCount": number }`. Returns at most the **50 most-recent** notifications (newest first); `unreadCount` counts ALL unread, not just the returned page. `type` ∈ `assigned | due_soon | overdue | ...`.

### POST /api/notifications/{id}
(also `PATCH`) Mark one notification read (owner-scoped). 200 on success, `404` if not the user's.

### POST /api/notifications/read-all
(also `PATCH`) Mark all of the user's notifications read. 200: `{ "ok": true, "count": number }` (`count` = number marked read). `401` if unauthenticated.

## Templates

Task templates: capture a task (and its sub-tasks + custom values) for reuse.

### GET /api/projects/{id}/templates
List templates available to a project (its own + its workspace scope).
- 200: `{ "templates": [{ "id", "name", "scope", "projectId", "workspaceId", "payload" (JSON string), "createdBy", "createdAt" }...] }`. `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/projects/{id}/templates
Create a template, OR instantiate one into the project (the body shape selects the path).
- **Create** body: `{ "name": string (trimmed, non-empty, max 120), "payload": { "task": { "title": string (required), "description"?: string|null, "priority"?, "recurrence"?: string|null, "fields"?: { "<fieldId>": string|null } }, "subtasks"?: [ <same task shape> ] }, "scope"?: "project" (default) | "workspace" }` → `201 { "id": string }`. Needs project structure-edit rights; a `scope:"workspace"` template ALSO needs workspace-edit rights — otherwise `404`. `400` on `name` missing/too long or `payload.task.title` missing.
- **Instantiate** body: `{ "templateId": string }` (or `{ "instantiate": true, "templateId": ... }`) → `201 { "taskId": string }` (the new top-level task id); creates the task + its sub-tasks + custom values. Only needs project access. `400` if `templateId` is missing or the template can't be instantiated.
- `400` on invalid JSON body; `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if the project is inaccessible.

### DELETE /api/templates/{id}
Delete a template. A PROJECT-scoped template needs project-edit rights; a WORKSPACE-scoped template needs workspace-edit rights. `404` if missing or inaccessible (incl. an orphaned template with neither scope); `403 "No edit permission on this template"` if accessible but not editable. 204: empty body.

## Export

### GET /api/projects/{id}/export?format=csv|json
Download a project. `format=csv` (default) = all tasks as CSV (UTF-8 BOM, CRLF, RFC-4180 quoting; columns: Title, Parent, Status, Priority, Assignee, Milestone, Due Date, one per custom field). `format=json` = a full-project document (`{ version, project, statuses, milestones, locations, labels, customFields, tasks }`, project-scoped structure only; statuses referenced by name) suitable for re-import via the project-settings Import. Access-gated (`404` otherwise); `400` for any other format; served as a `Content-Disposition: attachment` download. (Import itself is a web-only form action, not a REST endpoint.)

## Object shapes

- workspace: `id, name, ownerId, createdAt, updatedAt`.
- project: `id, name, description, workspaceId, statusId, icon, statusDisplay, pinned, startDate, dueDate, createdBy, createdAt, updatedAt` (`icon` = emoji or `iconoir:<name>` (nullable); `statusDisplay` ∈ `text|icon|text-icon` — the fallback default; per-view overrides live in `view.config.statusDisplay`; `chipFields` = a JSON-array string of project-entity custom-field ids (ordered) shown as header chips, or null = show all with a value; `startDate`/`dueDate` nullable ISO date strings)
- task: `id, projectId, parentId, title, description, statusId, priority, assigneeId, milestoneId, locationId, location, order, position, startDate, dueDate, recurrence, coverFileId, createdBy, createdAt, updatedAt` (`startDate`/`dueDate` nullable ISO dates; `recurrence` = repeat-rule string|null; `coverFileId` = a task-attachment file id|null), plus `customFields` (a `{ "<fieldId>": decodedValue }` map on API task responses). `locationId` → a saved project `location` (below; settable via POST/PATCH /api/tasks); `location` is the legacy freeform `"lat, lng"` text (still accepted; map views prefer `locationId`).
- location: `id, projectId, title, address, latitude, longitude, position, createdAt, updatedAt` — project-scoped saved places (`title` required; `address`/`latitude`/`longitude` nullable). Plotted by map views only when both lat+lng are set. Listed under `GET /api/projects/{id}`; reference one from a task via `locationId`. CRUD via the **Locations** endpoints (below).
- status: `id, name, description, category, color, icon, projectId, workspaceId, position, builtIn, createdAt`. Built-in ids → category: `status-backlog` (backlog), `status-planned` (planned), `status-in-progress` (in-progress), `status-completed` (completed), `status-canceled` (canceled).
- customField: `id, projectId, entity, name, type, config (JSON object), appliesTo, position, createdAt`. `entity` ∈ `task | project` (project-entity fields ARE returned in `GET /api/projects/{id}` `customFields`, but their values live on the project, not tasks). `type` ∈ `text|number|select|date|person|files|task|checkbox|email|phone|place|url|rollup` (`rollup` is computed client-side and never stored, so it never appears in a task's `customFields` map). `appliesTo` ∈ `all | tasks (top-level only) | subtasks` — a value can only be set on a task at the matching level (else `400`). `config` is type-specific: number `{numberFormat, currencyCode?, formatString?, rollupToParent?, rollupFormula?}` (`rollupToParent` (boolean) + `rollupFormula` (`count|sum|average|min|max`, default `sum`) make a parent task's value for the field a computed aggregate of its sub-tasks' values for the same field — display-only, honored only for `all`/`subtasks` fields, never stored on the parent); select `{multi, displayOption}`; date `{dateFormat, timeFormat}`; person/place/files/task `{multi}`; rollup `{relation, targetFieldId, formula}`.
- customFieldOption: `id, fieldId, title, color, icon, position, createdAt` — members of a `select` field.
- file: `id, projectId, fieldId, taskId, filename, mimeType, size, createdBy, createdAt` (the on-disk path is never exposed; fetch bytes via `GET /api/files/{id}`).
- view: `id, projectId, name, type (table|board|list|timeline|calendar|dashboard|map|flow), config (JSON string), position, isDefault, hidden, createdBy, createdAt, updatedAt`. `config` is schemaless per-view JSON; known **table/list** keys: `groupBy` (`status|milestone|assignee|due|label`; omit = ungrouped), `sortBy` (`priority|order|title|due|status|assignee|createdAt`, each optionally `:desc`; omit = natural order), `filters` (each array lists the **checked (shown)** values for that facet: `{ statusIds?, priorities?, assigneeIds?, milestoneIds?, labelIds?, dueBuckets? }`. A facet filters only when its key is **present**: absent = inactive = all shown; a present array shows only those values; an empty present array shows nothing; `_none` shows unassigned / no-milestone / no-label / no-due; a task is shown if ANY of its labels is checked), column toggles `priority|assignee|milestone|due|labels` and per-custom-field `cf:<fieldId>` (boolean; absent = shown; table only), `colWidths` (table only — `{ "<colKey>": px }`, colKey ∈ `status|title|priority|assignee|milestone|due|labels|cf:<fieldId>|actions`; presence switches the table to fixed-width columns), `statusIds` (array of status ids to show; absent = all), `showCount` (table grouped views — show the per-group task count; absent/`false` = hidden), `hideEmptyGroups` (default `true` = empty groups dropped; `false` shows them — table/list group sections; emptiness honors the view's filters/sort/shown-statuses). **board** keys: `groupBy` (`status` default `|milestone|assignee|label` — non-status values become swimlanes), `hideEmptyGroups` (default `true`; hides empty **swimlanes** when grouped by milestone/assignee/label — status COLUMNS are always shown as drop targets). **flow** keys: `sortBy`/`statusIds`/`filters` as above, plus `flowMilestones` (default `true`; `false` hides the milestone overlay + membership edges). `display` (`text|text-icon|icon`) applies to any view's tab; `statusDisplay` (`text|icon|text-icon`) is a **per-view** override of how the status pill renders (any view that shows statuses: table/board/list/flow/timeline/calendar), falling back to the project's `statusDisplay` default then `text`.
- milestone: `id, projectId, name, description, startDate, targetDate, position, createdAt, updatedAt` (`startDate`/`targetDate` nullable ISO dates). Returned under `GET /api/projects/{id}` and assignable via a task's `milestoneId`; CRUD + dependencies via the **Milestones** endpoints (below).
- label: `id, name, workspaceId, projectId, groupId, color, icon, position, createdAt` — exactly ONE of `workspaceId` (shared pool, attached to projects via `project_label`) / `projectId` (owned by one project) is set. `color` = `#rrggbb`|null; `icon` = emoji/`iconoir:<name>`|null; `groupId` → a label group|null.
- labelGroup: `id, name, workspaceId, position, createdAt` — a named grouping of workspace labels.
- apiKey (as listed by `GET /api/keys`): `id, name, prefix, lastUsedAt, createdAt` — `prefix` is the displayable start (e.g. `bsk_a1b2c3`); the hash and full token are never returned.

## Example

```
curl -X POST https://YOUR_HOST/api/tasks \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer bsk_xxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"projectId":"<id>","title":"Write the spec","priority":"high","status":"In progress"}'
```
